codeReview.summary: The packet stays tightly scoped to CI surfaces and covers the feature contract. `ci/pr_manifests/pr_auth_tenant.txt` now includes the canonical company-path HTTP regression, auth/runtime parity proof, stale admin-writer retirement proof, and OpenAPI contract proof; `scripts/ci_risk_router.py` routes both `openapi.json` and `OpenApiSnapshotIT` into `run_auth_tenant=true` without widening unrelated shards; and `testing/ci/test_pr_review_ci_packet.py` locks the company/admin/core-security routing cases plus the required auth-tenant manifest bundle. I did not find blocking correctness or contract-alignment issues in the reviewed diff.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared auth-hardening notes still describe `/api/v1/admin/tenant-runtime/policy` as an active peer in runtime-policy control tracking, even though Lane 01 retired that public writer. That stale note can mislead future workers reviewing auth/runtime behavior.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/auth-hardening.md:39 still says canonical company-path updates participate in the same policy-control tracking as `/api/v1/admin/tenant-runtime/policy`; `.factory/library/tenant-runtime-control-plane.md:18-21` documents that admin path as the stale writer that must stay retired.

sharedStateObservations.observation: `security-backend-worker` still treats a red-phase characterization failure as mandatory even for CI-only lane-routing packets. This review's handoff shows a justified deviation: manifest/router contract assertions plus shard execution were the realistic characterization path here.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/security-backend-worker/SKILL.md:33-39 requires characterization tests first; the handoff at /home/realnigga/.factory/missions/537a0e29-5ac5-4a24-8b7a-67bf56eaf048/handoffs/2026-03-15T02-57-21-668Z__lane01-route-regression-proof-into-pr-auth-tenant__3b4d917b-6464-4da4-84a2-a0433967df89.json:67-76 records `followedProcedure=false` and suggests explicitly allowing CI-only lane-routing packets to use router/manifest contract tests plus lane execution.

summary: PASS. The auth-tenant catching lane now includes the lane01 canonical runtime-policy regression bundle and the router/tests backstop that coverage without widening unrelated PR shards; I found no blocking implementation issues in this feature diff.
